# LoopGain

> Open-source cost control for AI agent loops. Stops each verify-revise loop once it has converged and rolls back before it degrades, instead of running to a fixed max_iterations=N cap — so you stop paying to iterate past a good answer. A Barkhausen-criterion (Aβ) trajectory classifier under the hood; framework- and model-agnostic.

LoopGain measures the loop gain Aβ of an iterative LLM loop in real time and classifies the loop's trajectory into one of five named states: FAST_CONVERGE, CONVERGING, STALLING, OSCILLATING, DIVERGING. The v0.3.0 classifier uses four trajectory features together — Aβ, statistical slope of the error trajectory, slope significance, and oscillation magnitude — and achieves 98.8% macro accuracy on a 1,000-trajectory deterministic-mock benchmark. The library is Python, Apache-2.0, zero-dependency until a framework adapter is installed.

The math is foundational electrical engineering control theory. Barkhausen (1921) established that a feedback loop's stability is entirely determined by its loop gain Aβ: if Aβ < 1 the loop converges, if Aβ > 1 the loop oscillates or diverges. An AI agent verify-revise loop is a feedback loop in the strict mathematical sense, so the same criterion applies. LoopGain is the first product extracted from a broader framework called ComponentAlgebra, which establishes a structural algebra between AI agent orchestration and electrical circuit design (36 EE-derived primitives, with a formal Turing-completeness proof on five of them).

## Install

- `pip install loopgain` — core library, zero-dependency
- `pip install loopgain[langgraph]` — adapter for LangGraph
- `pip install loopgain[crewai]` — adapter for CrewAI
- `pip install loopgain[autogen]` — adapter for AutoGen
- `pip install loopgain[langchain]` — adapter for LangChain (AgentExecutor)
- `pip install loopgain[openai-agents]` — adapter for OpenAI Agents SDK
- `pip install loopgain[claude-agent-sdk]` — adapter for Claude Agent SDK

## What it solves

- Three failure modes the classifier catches — STALLING (loop iterates but no longer makes progress, the most common silent waste), OSCILLATING (loop alternates between two states without converging), DIVERGING (each iteration is worse than the last, the rarest failure mode in practice)
- Replace `max_iterations=N` with a real measurement of whether the loop is converging instead of a hard counter
- Best-so-far rollback — return the lowest-error iteration when the loop has to stop
- Closed-form ETA prediction when the trajectory supports it
- Measured on a 2,000-trial pre-registered real-API benchmark: 93.5% cost reduction and ~10× speedup vs max_iterations=20, with output quality preserved (full methodology and raw data public)

## Docs

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/loopgain-ai/loopgain)
- [PyPI package](https://pypi.org/project/loopgain/)
- [Dashboard](https://dashboard.loopgain.ai) — six-panel telemetry dashboard for loop traces, band transitions, rollback events, and dollar-recoverable estimates
- [Telemetry endpoint](https://telemetry.loopgain.ai) — POST endpoint for the library to send anonymized trajectory data
- [Landing page](https://loopgain.ai)

## License & pricing

- Library, dashboard, and telemetry receiver are all open source under Apache-2.0
- Free if you self-host the receiver
- Paid hosted tier for the managed dashboard + telemetry receiver

## Optional

- [v2 classifier protocol](https://github.com/loopgain-ai/loopgain/blob/main/PROTOCOL_v2_classifier.md) — pre-registered four-feature trajectory classifier design with explicit thresholds and falsification criteria
- [v2 classifier validation results](https://github.com/loopgain-ai/loopgain/blob/main/RESULTS_v2_classifier.md) — 98.8% macro accuracy on N=1000 deterministic-mock benchmark with per-regime breakdown
- [README](https://github.com/loopgain-ai/loopgain/blob/main/README.md) — installation, quickstart, framework adapter examples
